findings.message: CRITICAL: CesiumGeoreference OriginHeight=800m is WGS84 ellipsoidal height, NOT AGL. Inhauma terrain elevation is ~795m; spawn altitude at 800m ellipsoidal = ~7m AGL. Aircraft spawns at ground level. Must be corrected to terrain_elevation + desired_AGL + geoid_separation (~2095m for 500m AGL spawn).

CRITICAL: Cesium for Unreal v2.21.1 is the last release supporting UE 5.4. All subsequent Cesium releases (v2.22.0+) require UE 5.5+. D1 (UE 5.4 pin) locks Cesium to an EOL version with no upstream bug fixes. Objection filed: consider upgrading to UE 5.5.

Google Maps ToS gray area: embedding Photorealistic 3D Tiles in a downloadable game binary is not explicitly authorized. Operator must obtain written confirmation before distribution. Cesium World Terrain + Bing Maps fallback avoids this risk entirely.

Google Maps $200/month promotional credit had published expiry of Feb 28, 2025. Operator must verify current credit status in GCP Console before any live tile session. Without credit, per-tile costs may approach or exceed $20 cap under heavy iteration.

OQ-07 closed by design decision: time of day fixed at midday UTC for screenshot-diff determinism. OQ-09, OQ-11 closed: own-work primitive BP construction for aircraft and AA gun. OQ-10 closed: Cesium for Unreal pinned to v2.21.1.

Airstrip placed at lat -19.45, lon -44.50 (NW farmland, fictional farm strip). AA gun placed at lat -19.490, lon -44.387 (near church on high ground). Both positions use real geo-coordinates requiring CesiumGeoreference inverse-transform for UE5 world placement.

decisions_required: Operator: confirm Google Maps ToS permits embedding Photorealistic 3D Tiles in downloadable game binary.
Operator: verify $200/month GCP credit is still active for their account.
Operator/software-architect: decide whether to keep UE 5.4 (Cesium v2.21.1 EOL) or upgrade to UE 5.5 (unlocks current Cesium v2.26.0).
Operator: clarify intended CesiumGeoreference OriginHeight semantics (WGS84 ellipsoidal vs AGL vs terrain-relative).
game-tester (OQ-06): screenshot-diff baseline imagery may violate Google Maps ToS — legal review or alternative approach required before freezing baselines.
